title: VERA: Turning Video Models into Generalist Robot Policies
summary: Video generative models can depict the completion of complex tasks across embodiments and environments, and recent robot foundation models finetune them on action-labeled data to jointly predict future observations and actions. VERA (Video-to-Embodied Robot Action Model) instead leaves the video planner as-is and trains only an embodiment-specific inverse dynamics model (IDM) based on the robot embodiment Jacobian — keeping the planner embodiment-agnostic and interchangeable, and letting the IDM train on readily available self-play data. The resulting closed-loop, video-to-action policy is data-efficient and scales to high-dimensional action spaces, achieving strong results across simulation and the real world, including zero-shot Panda arm manipulation and 16-DoF Allegro-hand dexterous cube re-orientation. The same video planner transfers across embodiments by pairing it with different IDMs, positioning decoupled video planning plus faithful video-to-action translation as a route to zero-shot, cross-embodiment robot control.
